g.text: Valuation & Conclusions
802 Bankview Dr, Drumheller, Alberta | File VAL251026
Capitalization Rate Analysis – Multifamily
The subject property, Binscarth Apartments at 802 Bankview Dr in Drumheller, is a 24-unit walk-up apartment
complex built in 1981 with average-quality finishes and stable in-place income. The property generates NOI of
approximately $8,659.00 per unit. Due to the lack of recent multifamily transactions in Drumheller, comparable sales
from similar secondary and tertiary Alberta markets were used. The four comparables reflect cap rates ranging from
6.02% to 7.10%, with an average of approximately 6.48%.
Comp 1 (639 Westmount Dr Apartment, Strathmore, 2024, 6.02%): A 12-unit property built in 1973 in a slightly
stronger market than Drumheller. NOI of $10,291.00 per unit is moderately higher than the subject, reflecting
somewhat stronger rents and demand. The 6.02% cap rate sits at the low end of the range and is indicative of small-
scale assets in secondary markets with stable income and modest upside.
Comp 2 (24 19 St NE Apartments, Medicine Hat, 2025, 6.04%): A 12-unit 1975 building with NOI of $6,541.00 per unit,
below the subject’s level. Despite weaker income, it traded at a similar 6.04% cap rate, suggesting investors are pricing
in some potential for improved performance but still requiring a yield consistent with tertiary-market risk.
Comp 3 (6547 59 Ave Apartment, Red Deer, 2024, 7.10%): A 10-unit 2001-built property with superior amenities and
the highest NOI in the set at $11,289.00 per unit. The 7.10% cap rate is the highest in the data, reflecting investor
expectations for higher returns on small assets, even in larger regional centres, and potential concerns around small
scale and income durability.
Comp 4 (Spruce Court, Redwater, 2025 Listing, 6.77%): A 12-unit 1980-built property in a small tertiary market, with
NOI of $10,824.00 per unit. The listing guidance implies a 6.77% cap rate, consistent with investor requirements for
average-quality walk-up apartments in very small Alberta communities, where liquidity and demand are more limited.
Across the dataset, the subject property is expected to warrant a cap rate near the middle of the observed range. Key
considerations include:
• NOI per unit that is generally in line with the comp set
• Average-quality finishes and 1981 construction similar to several comparables
• Location in a tertiary Alberta market with investor expectations comparable to Strathmore, Medicine Hat,
Red Deer fringe, and Redwater
• Most cap rates clustering between 6.00% and 7.00%, with tighter support around the low- to mid-6.00%
range for stabilized assets with some upside
For the As Is valuation, a 6.25% cap rate was selected to reflect the subject’s below-market in-place rents and the
associated upside potential. Investors typically accept a lower going-in cap rate when there is clear room to increase
rents and grow NOI, as this embedded upside compensates for a reduced initial yield.
For the As If Renovated and Stabilized valuation, a higher 6.50% cap rate was applied because, under this scenario,
the renovation program brings rents up to market levels with limited further upside. Once an asset is fully stabilized
at market rents, investors generally require a higher cap rate to achieve their target returns, given the diminished
prospects for additional income growth.
Based on these factors, the subject’s market-supported cap rate is best positioned in the 6.25% to 6.50% range,
depending on whether it is analyzed on an As Is or As If Renovated and Stabilized basis.
